front: Novel: set on the fourteenth birthday of John Grimes, a preacher's son who has a spiritual rebirth at the Temple of the Fire Baptized
back: Go Tell It on the Mountain (James Baldwin)
work: Go Tell It on the Mountain

front: Novel: 'The Threshing-Floor' is the third section depicting the protagonist's religious awakening; Gabriel is revealed not to be his true father
back: Go Tell It on the Mountain (James Baldwin)
work: Go Tell It on the Mountain

front: Novel: the narrator David recounts his affair with an Italian bartender in Paris who is guillotined for murdering the bar owner Guillaume
Short Story: a heroin-addicted jazz musician is taken in by his math-teacher brother; ends with a glass of Scotch and milk shaking like 'the very cup of trembling'
Short Story: the title character suggests 'maybe it was good to smell your own stink' while slowly crushing an empty beer can
Short Story: one character is convinced to live at Isabel's house after learning she owns a piano; he plays with Creole's band at a Greenwich Village club
Author: essay 'Everybody's Protest Novel' attacks Uncle Tom's Cabin and argues that Bigger Thomas 'accepted a theology that denies him life'
Author: title essay of a collection describes his father's death coinciding with the Detroit race riots; he throws a water jug at a waitress who refused to serve him
Author: 'Down at the Cross' describes dinner with Elijah Muhammad; calls finding religion a 'gimmick' to escape racism
Author: collection containing 'My Dungeon Shook,' a letter to his nephew contending 'the country is celebrating one hundred years of freedom one hundred years too soon'
Novel: the sculptor Fonny is falsely accused of rape, separating him from his pregnant fiancee Tish; adapted into a 2019 Barry Jenkins film
back: If Beale Street Could Talk (James Baldwin)

front: Author: unfinished manuscript Remember This House, about Malcolm X, MLK, and Medgar Evers, became the documentary I Am Not Your Negro by Raoul Peck
work: I Am Not Your Negro / Remember This House

front: Author: debated William F. Buckley at Cambridge in 1965 on 'Is the American Dream at the Expense of the American Negro?'; won by consensus
Author: book-length film criticism analyzing the racial subtext of The Exorcist and the loyal maid archetype from Birth of a Nation to Guess Who's Coming to Dinner
Author: criticized the all-Black Carmen Jones for its 'sterile and distressing eroticism' and for being 'at pains to desexualize Harry Belafonte'
Novel: the jazz musician Rufus Scott jumps off the George Washington Bridge
